prose:
The Fukui reactivity analysis of aniline was completed successfully. The calculation was performed using Density Functional Theory (DFT) with the B3LYP functional and the def2-TZVP basis set (tier 'standard') in the gas phase, for the neutral, singlet state (charge 0, multiplicity 1). The analysis was carried out using the `fukui-reactivity` skill in `chemkit`, which uses PySCF as the backend DFT engine. The calculation integrity is trustworthy, with all charge conservation checks passing.

The results indicate:
*   The site most susceptible to **electrophilic attack** (where a nucleophile would attack) is atom C3, with the largest Fukui(+) index of **2.2976**.
*   The site most susceptible to **nucleophilic attack** (where an electrophile would attack) is atom N1, with the largest Fukui(-) index of **0.20452**.

A summary of the condensed Fukui indices is below (f+ for electrophilic attack, f- for nucleophilic attack, and f0 for radical attack):

| Atom | Symbol | f+ | f- | f0 |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 1 | N | 1.6697 | 0.20452 | 0.93709 |
| 2 | C | -1.9009 | -0.0037261 | -0.95232 |
| 3 | C | 2.2976 | 0.066571 | 1.1821 |
| 4 | C | 2.2966 | 0.066589 | 1.1816 |
| 5 | C | -0.28837 | 0.0083459 | -0.14001 |
| 6 | C | -0.28785 | 0.0083791 | -0.13973 |
| 7 | C | 1.4032 | 0.10973 | 0.75645 |
| 8 | H | -0.35139 | 0.081996 | -0.13470 |
| 9 | H | -0.35134 | 0.082014 | -0.13466 |
| 10 | H | -0.82107 | 0.076355 | -0.37236 |
| 11 | H | -0.82131 | 0.076346 | -0.37248 |
| 12 | H | -0.34269 | 0.087366 | -0.12766 |
| 13 | H | -0.75088 | 0.067758 | -0.34156 |
| 14 | H | -0.75116 | 0.067751 | -0.34171 |
